title: EMBER.ASH.REST.BBS
subtitle: Cicadas at Dusk · Vol 5 · Ch V · The Hierophant
notes: Sysop's converted basement — the modem rack, the green-screen CRT, the wall of floppies, the printer's banner paper. People come here online and they come here in person. The basement door opens onto the alley behind the music store. Ten spaces.
ambient_pool: the modem rack hums. it has not stopped humming since 1996.
the CRT's green phosphor is the only light in this corner of the room.
somebody on the BBS is typing. you can hear the printer's banner paper roll.
the printer's banner paper has been collecting for three days.
the cicadas outside the alley door are loud tonight.
the floppy wall hums faintly. it's not supposed to.

spaces.label: ALLEY DOOR
spaces.flavor: the basement's outside door — opens into the alley behind the music store.
arrival_lines: the alley door is open a crack. you didn't open it.
the cicadas outside the alley door are louder than the modem fan.

spaces.label: STAIRS UP
spaces.flavor: the stairs up to the music store · the second leap path.
arrival_lines: the stairs creak. they always have.
the door at the top is unlocked. the music store closed two hours ago.

spaces.label: MODEM WINDOW
spaces.flavor: the small basement window high on the wall · the third leap path.
arrival_lines: the window looks out at street level. feet go by.
the window is small. you would need to be small too.

spaces.label: CRT TERMINAL
spaces.flavor: sysop's seat · green-phosphor screen · the modem on standby.
arrival_lines: the CRT is on. the terminal prompt is ready.
the modem chirps in standby. somebody is about to dial in.

spaces.label: MODEM RACK
spaces.flavor: the rack of four modems · the BBS's lungs.
arrival_lines: four modems, four phone lines. all four are lit.
the rack hums. it knows you're here.

spaces.label: FLOPPY WALL
spaces.flavor: two thousand 3.5" floppies · the BBS's archive · alphabetised, indexed.
arrival_lines: the wall is taller than you are. the index card next to it has your handwriting on it.
you find a floppy you don't remember labeling. it's in your hand.

spaces.label: PRINTER TABLE
spaces.flavor: the dot-matrix printer + the box of fan-fold banner paper.
arrival_lines: the printer has stopped mid-page. somebody's post got cut.
the banner paper has been rolling all night. there's a stack of it on the floor.

spaces.label: BACK KITCHEN
spaces.flavor: a small alcove with a kettle, two mugs, instant coffee, hot sauce.
arrival_lines: the kettle is on. it might've been on all evening.
the hot sauce bottle is open.

spaces.label: COUCH CORNER
spaces.flavor: an L-shaped couch by the alley door · where visitors who showed up in person sit.
arrival_lines: the couch has a blanket on it. it has been used recently.
the corner-cushion remembers everyone who's been there.

spaces.label: HAM RADIO
spaces.flavor: the ham radio set sysop got from his uncle · still works · still legal.
arrival_lines: the ham radio is on standby. the green dial is at 14.301 MHz.
somebody on 14.230 is talking about the weather. the weather is fine.

signal_track.label: SIGNAL
signal_track.notes: Sysop's positive doom-clock. TRANSMIT and RECEIVE actions tick Signal up. At threshold the BBS is at peak attendance and the room's energy is humming.

ladder.label: the BBS is quiet. four modems on standby.
halfway · the printer is keeping up.
the room is humming with conversations.
one more user and the BBS is at peak.
peak signal · the BBS is the city tonight.
